The Open Agentic Commerce Stack – a16z crypto (Eddy Lazzarin, Sam Ragsdale, Noah Levine, Robert Hackett)

2 min read · tweet / X post · 2026-05-04
ai-agentsagentic-commercecryptoa16zinternet-business-modelsautonomous-agentspaymentsopen-protocolsweb3machine-economy
Source: https://x.com/a16zcrypto/status/2048811687864901801?s=46&t=OHfXTbjdV4tiD3nnG1lsT...

Summary

This post from a16z crypto introduces the concept of an 'open agentic commerce stack'—a framework for what happens when AI agents begin transacting autonomously on behalf of humans. The core thesis is that as agents become capable of performing nearly any computer-based task, the natural next step is enabling them to spend money, negotiate, and engage in commerce, which will fundamentally rewire the internet's existing business model built around human attention and clicks. The framing suggests that today's internet monetization layer—advertising, subscriptions, walled gardens—is ill-suited for a world where machines, not humans, are the primary actors browsing, comparing, and purchasing. An open stack implies the need for permissionless, programmable payment rails (likely crypto-native) that agents can use without traditional gatekeepers, positioning blockchain infrastructure as a critical enabler of machine-to-machine commerce. By assembling voices from across a16z crypto's research and editorial teams (Lazzarin, Ragsdale, Levine, Hackett), the post signals that the firm views agentic commerce not as a speculative future but as an imminent architectural shift. The emphasis on 'open' is deliberate: it argues against proprietary agent-commerce platforms and in favor of interoperable protocols, which aligns with a16z's broader crypto investment thesis around open networks and decentralized infrastructure.

Key Takeaways

  • AI agents are approaching full capability parity with humans for computer-based tasks, making autonomous commerce a near-term reality.
  • The internet's current business model (ads, subscriptions, human-centric UX) is structurally mismatched for agent-driven interactions and will need to be rewired.
  • An 'open agentic commerce stack' is proposed—permissionless, interoperable protocols that let agents transact without centralized gatekeepers.
  • Crypto and blockchain payment rails are positioned as natural infrastructure for machine-to-machine payments due to their programmability and openness.
  • a16z crypto frames this not as a distant vision but as an imminent architectural shift requiring new protocol-level design now.
  • Proprietary agent-commerce platforms risk recreating the walled-garden problems of today's internet; open protocols are the preferred alternative.
  • The shift from human-attention economies to agent-transaction economies could redistribute value across the entire internet stack.

Full Text / Transcript

Agents can now do almost anything a human can do with a computer. So what happens when they start spending money on your behalf? @eddylazzarin, @samrags_, @nlevine19, and @rhackett on the open agentic commerce stack, and why the internet's business model is about to get rewired. https://t.co/M485vJ9IYB
